title: Schizoloma griffithianum
taxonomy: Schizoloma griffithianum is classified as a species of fern in the family Lindsaeaceae. It is a type of climbing fern native to Sri Lanka. The classification of Schizoloma griffithianum is further divided into the genus Schizoloma and the species S. griffithianum. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and morphological features. Schizoloma griffithianum is an example of a fern that has been studied and classified by botanists.

intro: Schizoloma griffithianum, also known as Griffith's forked fern or Griffith's spleenwort, is a species of fern in the family Aspleniaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Schizoloma griffithianum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Schizoloma griffithianum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of herbal teas, tinctures, and capsules.
